steps.title: Identify the y-intercept location
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the line crosses the y-axis. This always occurs when $x = 0$.

steps.title: Read the y-value from the graph
steps.content:
Locate $x = 0$ on the graph (the vertical y-axis). The line crosses the y-axis at $y = 5$. Therefore, the y-intercept is the point $(0, 5)$.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
